clauses.title: Debt-to-Income (DTI) Threshold
clauses.text: Debt-to-Income (DTI) ratio, calculated as the applicant's monthly loan annuity divided by monthly income, must not exceed 50% for Personal Loan approval. Applications with a DTI ratio above this threshold cannot be auto-approved and require referral to a human underwriter or an outright decline based on compensating factors.

clauses.title: Minimum Income Requirement
clauses.text: Applicants for a Personal Loan must demonstrate a minimum verified annual income of $15,000 (or local currency equivalent). Applications where verified income falls below this minimum income floor cannot be approved and must be referred for manual underwriting review.

clauses.title: Loan-to-Income (LTI) Cap
clauses.text: The total requested credit amount must not exceed 6 times the applicant's verified annual income (a Loan-to-Income, or LTI, cap of 6.0x) for Personal Loan products. Requests exceeding this cap require additional collateral, a co-signer, or explicit human underwriter override before approval.

clauses.title: Thin-File Escalation Rule
clauses.text: Applicants with fewer than 24 months of employment history, no prior credit bureau record, or an unemployment/pension sentinel value in the employment-duration field are classified as thin-file. Thin-file applications must be escalated to a human underwriter for manual review regardless of the model-predicted risk score or band.

clauses.title: Fairness Guardrail
clauses.text: No recommendation may be based on, or materially influenced by, an applicant's gender, age, marital status, or other protected characteristic. Any model feature or policy rule found to correlate with a protected attribute above the fairness guardrail threshold must be excluded from automated decisioning and flagged for fairness audit before it can be used.
